steps.title: Identify the question type
steps.content: This is a main-purpose question. We need the overall point of the passage, not a detail it incidentally contains.

steps.title: Trace the structure
steps.content: The text first describes dopamine-based signaling in mammals, then uses "but" to introduce Huang's new observation that honeybees also show a dopamine-based signaling process. The whole point is the parallel: a brain mechanism long described in mammals also appears in an insect.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'experimental method of measuring... responses in humans': the passage neither describes a method nor focuses on humans. ✗
  • Choice B — 'processes by which certain insects can express how they are feeling': misreads the passage. Huang observes the underlying signaling mechanism, not insect expression of feelings. ✗
  • Choice D — 'certain insects and mammals behave similarly when there is a possibility of a reward': emphasizes shared behavior, but the passage's punch line is the shared physiological mechanism (dopamine signaling), not behavioral similarity. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
